turns.content: What brings you in today?
That burning pain in my stomach, right after I eat. It's been going on for a few months now.
When did this start?
Not sure exactly. Maybe three or four months ago? It's been getting worse, though.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?
It's right here, in the middle of my upper stomach. Like a sharp, burning ache. Comes on fast after meals.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No, nothing like that. No fever. Sometimes I feel queasy, but it passes if I lie down for a bit.
What medications are you currently taking?
Just some ibuprofen now and then, if my back acts up from standing all day. Nothing else regular.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
No, not really. I had some acid reflux a few years back, but the doctor said it was mild. Haven't taken anything for it in ages.
Are you allergic to any medications?
Don't think so. Never had a problem with any meds.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?
Yeah, I've been trying antacids from the pharmacy. They help a little at first, but the pain always comes back. Doesn't really do much after a while.
Does anything make it better or worse?
Lying down helps some. But eating anything spicy or greasy makes it a lot worse. That's tough in my line of work.
How has this been affecting your daily life?
It's making work miserable. I'm a chef, so I'm always tasting and prepping food. Hard to concentrate when my stomach's on fire. I've been snapping at the line cooks more too.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
I smoke maybe half a pack a day. Drink most nights, a few beers after my shift. Nothing crazy, but yeah, I drink.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?
My dad had ulcers, I think. Never really talked about it much, but he was always popping antacids when I was a kid.
Have you experienced anything like this before?
Yeah, I had heartburn on and off a few years back. But nothing like this—never this constant or this bad. And that antacid used to knock it right out.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?
Physically right now, it's maybe a 4. That burning's there, but it's not the worst it's been. Emotionally? I'm pretty fed up. This thing's been dragging on for months. I'd say a 7. I'm tired of it.
Is there anything else you think I should know?
No, I think that's about it. Just the pain after eating and nothing seems to help much anymore.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
I just try to get through my shifts, honestly. Pop a few antacids between courses and hope it settles. But I can't keep doing this. That's why I'm here.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
I told you—just push through my shifts and take those antacids. Not much else I can do.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
Like I said, I just get on with it. But it's not really working, is it? That's why I came.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
Look, I told you already. I just deal with it day to day. Can we move on? I need some help here, not the same question over and over.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
I've said it three times now. I push through work and take antacids. That's it. Can we please talk about what's actually wrong with me?
